programmes.show_description: (2022) Horror prequel with Isabelle Fuhrman and Julia Stiles. Diminutive psychopath Esther - think Wednesday at a funeral - cons her way into a family to wreak murderous mayhem.
...Peculiar Children: (2016) Eva Green and Asa Butterfield star in Tim Burton's fantasy adventure. A teenager enters the magical, but scary and violent, world where his grandfather grew up.

programmes.show_name: The Kid Who Would Be King
programmes.show_description: (2019) Fantasy adventure. A 12-year-old from modern-day suburbia finds King Arthur's sword Excalibur and must save the country from a sinister sorceress. With Patrick Stewart.
(1971) Vanessa Redgrave stars in a dramatisation of the life of the 16th-century Scottish queen. Glenda Jackson co-stars as her cousin and rival, England's Queen Elizabeth I.
(2019) Greta Gerwig's Oscar-winning period drama adaptation of the classic Louisa May Alcott novel, starring Emma Watson, Saoirse Ronan, Florence Pugh, Timothée Chalamet and Meryl Streep.
...Interview Special: Stars Chris Hemsworth and Tessa Thompson, director F Gary Gray and producers Walter Parkes and Laurie Macdonald talk about bringing the series back for a fourth part.
(2019) Fourth instalment of the sci-fi comedy franchise with Chris Hemsworth, Tessa Thompson and Liam Neeson. An alien-obsessed rookie joins London's Men in Black team.
(2019) Keanu Reeves returns for the third time as the supposedly retired hitman. Someone's put a contract on John Wick's head and only a mystic in Morocco can lift it.
(2021) Dystopian crime thriller sequel. The NFFA are back. The Purge is back. Racists feel empowered. And when migrants start to go missing in Texas, it seems like the Purge will never end.
(2023) Gothic horror with Max Eigenmann. A Filipina cleaner takes a job in a big, creepy house so she and her daughter can stay in the UK. But there's an old man in a coma in the attic...

Four children compete in two crafty upcycling challenges working with recycled metal. But only two will make it through to the semi-finals of Crafty Champions.

(1957) Western. A Union army deserter sets out to turn his Texan hometown's womenfolk into sharpshooters in order to repel a Comanche attack. Starring Audie Murphy and Kathryn Grant.
...Interview Special: Stars Chris Hemsworth and Tessa Thompson, director F Gary Gray and producers Walter Parkes and Laurie Macdonald talk about bringing the series back for a fourth part.
(1951) Second World War thriller. Richard Widmark, Dana Andrews and Robert Wagner star as divers with a special underwater demolition team taking the war to the Japanese in the Pacific.
(1953) Fact-based Second World War adventure. Richard Widmark stars as a US Navy officer who leads an alliance of scientists and tribesmen against the Japanese in Mongolia.
(1954) Douglas Sirk's melodrama starring Rock Hudson and Jane Wyman. A playboy reforms his lifestyle in order to seek forgiveness from a woman whose husband's death he inadvertently caused.
(1999) Action thriller starring Sean Connery as Robert 'Mac' MacDougal, one of the world's finest art thieves, and Catherine Zeta-Jones as the insurance company agent out to trap him.
(2000) Fact-based legal drama. Julia Roberts won an Oscar, Golden Globe and BAFTA for her portrayal of a single mum making a huge impact at a legal firm despite having no formal training.
